name: get_cluster_info
description: Get Cohesity cluster information including name, ID, software version, and node count

name: get_cluster_stats
description: Get Cohesity cluster storage statistics including total capacity, used and available bytes, and data protection usage

name: list_protection_policies
description: List all Cohesity data protection policies including schedule, retention, and replication settings

name: create_protection_policy
description:
Create a new Cohesity protection policy. Supports:
- Basic: incremental-only with retention (e.g. daily incremental, keep 30 days)
- GFS (Grandfather-Father-Son): incremental + weekly full + extended retention tiers (weekly/monthly/yearly). GFS requires full_schedule_day_of_week and at least one of gfs_weekly_retention/gfs_monthly_retention/gfs_yearly_retention.
- DataLock: WORM protection that prevents snapshot deletion. When enabled, applies to all retention tiers automatically.
- Retry options and description also supported.

name: update_protection_policy
description: Update an existing Cohesity protection policy. Fetches the current policy and applies changes. Use list_protection_policies to get the policy ID.

name: delete_protection_policy
description: Delete a Cohesity protection policy. The policy must not be in use by any protection groups.

name: list_protection_groups
description: List Cohesity protection groups (backup jobs) with status, schedule, and last run information

name: get_protection_group
description: Get detailed information about a specific Cohesity protection group including configuration, schedule, and last run status

name: create_protection_group
description: Create a new Cohesity protection group (backup job). For VMware, provide vm_ids to protect specific VMs. Requires a policy ID and storage domain ID.

name: update_protection_group
description: Update a Cohesity VMware protection group to add or remove VMs. Fetches the current group state, applies the changes, and saves. Use search_objects to find VM IDs first.

name: delete_protection_group
description: Delete a Cohesity protection group. Optionally delete all associated snapshots.

name: pause_protection_group
description: Pause a Cohesity protection group so it stops running scheduled backups

name: resume_protection_group
description: Resume a paused Cohesity protection group so it resumes scheduled backups

name: run_protection_group
description: Trigger an on-demand backup run for a Cohesity protection group

name: list_protection_runs
description: List recent backup runs for a Cohesity protection group with status, duration, and data size

name: get_protection_run
description: Get detailed information about a specific Cohesity backup run including per-object status and statistics

name: list_sources
description: List all registered Cohesity protection sources such as vSphere, Physical servers, NAS, SQL, and more

name: search_objects
description: Search for Cohesity protectable objects (VMs, physical servers, databases, etc.) by name or environment. The object ID used for protection groups is found in objectProtectionInfos[0].objectId in the response. For a full list of all VMware objects with IDs, use list_sources then get_source.

name: get_source
description: Get detailed information about a specific Cohesity protection source including its object hierarchy

name: refresh_source
description: Refresh a registered protection source to sync the latest inventory from it. For vCenter, this re-discovers VMs, datastores, hosts, and clusters. Use list_sources to get the source ID.

name: register_vmware_source
description: Register a VMware vCenter, ESXi standalone host, or vCloud Director endpoint as a backup source

name: register_physical_source
description: Register a physical server (Linux, Windows, AIX, Solaris, SAP HANA) as a backup source. Requires the Cohesity agent to be installed and reachable on the host.

name: register_azure_source
description: Register an Azure tenant or subscription as a backup source for protecting Azure VMs, SQL, Files, Blob, and other workloads

name: register_aws_source
description: Register an AWS account as a backup source for protecting EC2, RDS, S3, DynamoDB, and other AWS workloads

name: register_m365_source
description: Register a Microsoft 365 tenant as a backup source for protecting Exchange Online, OneDrive, SharePoint, Teams, and Groups

name: register_nas_source
description: Register a generic NAS mount point (NFS3, NFS4.1, or SMB/CIFS) as a backup source

name: update_source_registration
description: Update an existing source registration (rotate credentials, change endpoint, etc.). For VMware vCenter only — for other env types, use the raw API.

name: unregister_source
description: Unregister (delete) a source registration. WARNING: removes the source from Cohesity but does not delete existing backups.

name: list_recovery_tasks
description: List Cohesity recovery tasks with status, type, and progress information

name: get_recovery_task
description: Get detailed information about a specific Cohesity recovery task including per-object restore status

name: list_alerts
description: List Cohesity cluster alerts with severity, description, and resolution status

name: resolve_alert
description: Mark a Cohesity alert as resolved

name: list_storage_domains
description: List Cohesity storage domains (view boxes) where backups are stored. The storage domain ID is required when creating protection groups.

name: list_objects
description: List protectable objects under a registered source (e.g., all VMs under a vCenter). Returns objects with direct 'id' fields suitable for use in protection groups. Requires a parentId (source or container ID from list_sources).

name: list_snapshots
description: List available backup snapshots for a specific object (VM, database, etc.). Use the object ID from search_objects (objectProtectionInfos[0].objectId) or list_objects. Snapshot IDs are required for recovery operations.

name: browse_snapshot_files
description: Browse files and folders in a VM snapshot using Cohesity's indexed file search. Returns immediate children of the specified path. Requires indexing to be enabled on the protection group. Use object_id from search_objects (objectProtectionInfos[0].objectId). If indexing is not enabled, enable it via update_protection_group first.

name: recover_vm
description: Recover a VMware VM from a Cohesity snapshot. By default restores to the original location (overwrite). Use list_snapshots to find the snapshot ID. For in-place recovery, only snapshot_id and name are required.

name: search_files
description: Search for files and folders across Cohesity snapshots by name. Returns matching files with their paths, source VM, and snapshot info. NOTE: Cohesity prefixes VMware file paths with a volume label (e.g. 'lvol_2/home/user/file') — pass these paths as-is to recover_files, which will automatically strip the prefix when restoring to the original location.

name: recover_files
description: Recover specific files or folders from a VMware VM snapshot back to the original VM. Use list_snapshots to get a snapshot ID and search_files to find file paths. Pass file paths exactly as returned by search_files (e.g. 'lvol_2/home/user/file') — the volume prefix is automatically stripped when restoring to original path. The recover_method 'UseExistingAgent' requires a Cohesity agent installed on the VM; 'UseHypervisorApis' uses VMware Tools (requires VM credentials).

name: get_protection_heatmap
description: Generate a Protected Objects Heatmap report showing per-VM/object backup status for each day in a date range — matching the Cohesity GUI Reports > Protected Objects Heatmap view. Returns a summary (total runs, success, failed, canceled) and a per-object daily grid with run details (protection group, start time, duration, logical size, data read, status).

name: get_protection_summary
description: Generate a protection job summary report showing overall backup health: success/failure counts, SLA violations, and a list of failing protection groups with their last run status. Good for daily operational review.

name: get_recovery_summary
description: Generate a recovery activity summary showing how many objects were recovered, recovery task statuses, and recent recovery operations in a time window.

name: list_external_targets
description: List all registered external targets (cloud vaults, tape, NAS) used for archival and tiering. Returns target IDs needed for protection policy archival configuration.

name: get_external_target
description: Get details of a specific external target by ID.

name: create_external_target_aws
description: Register a new AWS S3 external target for archival or tiering. Requires an existing IAM role or access key credentials.

name: create_external_target_azure
description: Register a new Azure Blob Storage external target for archival or tiering.

name: delete_external_target
description: Delete a registered external target. Use force_delete=true only if the target has no active archival jobs.

name: list_tiering_tasks
description: List all data tiering tasks that move cold data from primary storage to external targets (cloud, vault). Shows status, schedule, and source/target configuration.

name: get_tiering_task
description: Get details of a specific data tiering task by ID.

name: create_tiering_task
description: Create a data tiering task to automatically move cold data from a NAS/View source to an external target. Requires a registered external target (use list_external_targets to find IDs).

name: run_tiering_task
description: Trigger an on-demand run of a data tiering task.

name: update_tiering_task_state
description: Pause or resume one or more data tiering tasks.

name: delete_tiering_task
description: Delete a data tiering task.

name: list_notification_rules
description: List all alert notification rules. Each rule defines which alert categories/severities trigger email, SNMP, syslog, or webhook notifications.

name: create_notification_rule
description: Create an alert notification rule to send emails or webhooks when specific alert categories or severities occur.

name: update_notification_rule
description: Update an existing alert notification rule by ID.

name: delete_notification_rule
description: Delete an alert notification rule by ID.

name: get_cluster_storage_stats
description: Get current cluster storage statistics including total capacity, used space, data reduction ratios, and storage breakdown by category. Useful for capacity planning.

name: get_workload_stats
description: Get high-level workload statistics showing data volumes and counts per workload type (VMware, Physical, NAS, SQL, etc.) on the cluster.

name: get_replication_backlog
description: Get replication backlog statistics showing how much data is pending replication to remote clusters. Useful for monitoring replication health.

name: get_replication_clusters
description: Get list of remote replication clusters with total data replicated to/from each. Shows replication partner health at a glance.

name: get_replication_data_trend
description: Get replication data transfer trends over a time range. Returns time-series data showing how much data was replicated at each interval. Useful for capacity planning and SLA reporting.

name: get_replication_objects
description: List all objects that have been replicated to remote clusters in the given time range, with per-object replication status and data size.

name: list_audit_logs
description: Query the cluster audit log. Filter by user, action, entity type, time window, or free-text search. Returns who-did-what-when records for compliance and forensics.

name: list_audit_log_actions
description: List all action types recognized by the audit log (for building filters)

name: list_audit_log_entity_types
description: List all entity types tracked in the audit log (for building filters)

name: get_protected_objects_trend_report
description: Get a per-object backup success/failure trend report over a time window. Returns each protected object with daily or weekly rollups of total, successful, running, cancelled, and failed runs.

name: get_sources_jobs_summary_report
description: Get a report mapping protection sources to the protection groups (jobs) covering them, with run counts per source.

name: get_archival_transfer_report
description: Get a report of data transferred to external archival targets (vaults) in a given time window.

name: generate_protection_summary_report
description: Generate a synthesized Markdown protection summary report: protection groups, their last run status, and any active alerts. Combines multiple V2 endpoints into one human-readable view.

name: generate_failed_backups_report
description: Generate a Markdown report listing protection groups whose most recent run failed, was missed, or finished with warnings. Useful for daily backup health triage.

name: generate_capacity_report
description: Generate a Markdown capacity report showing cluster storage usage, available capacity, deduplication ratio, and data reduction stats.

name: cancel_protection_run
description: Cancel a running protection group run. Can cancel the entire run or a subset of objects/copies (local snapshot, archival, replication). Already-completed object tasks are not cancelled.

name: cancel_recovery_task
description: Cancel an in-flight recovery task by its ID. Returns 204 No Content on success.

name: set_snapshot_datalock
description: Apply DataLock (WORM) protection to a protection group run's local snapshot. Once locked, the snapshot cannot be deleted until its retention expires. Compliance lock is even more restrictive — it cannot be released by an administrator.

name: set_snapshot_legal_hold
description: Place a snapshot on legal hold (or release it). While on hold, the snapshot cannot be deleted regardless of retention policy. Requires Data Security Role.

name: extend_snapshot_retention
description: Extend or shorten a snapshot's retention. Positive days_delta extends, negative shortens. If the resulting expiry falls before now, the snapshot is deleted immediately.

name: delete_snapshot
description: Delete a protection group run's local snapshot immediately. WARNING: irreversible. Will fail if the snapshot is under DataLock or Legal Hold.

name: list_users
description: List Cohesity users. Filterable by domain, sid, username (partial or exact), email, or role.

name: get_user
description: Get a single Cohesity user by SID

name: create_local_user
description: Create a new LOCAL Cohesity user with a password. For AD/IdP users, use create_ad_user.

name: create_ad_user
description: Create a Cohesity user mapped to an existing Active Directory or IdP principal. No password is set on Cohesity — auth flows through the identity provider.

name: update_user
description: Update a Cohesity user — roles, description, restricted flag, or LOCAL user password.

name: delete_users
description: Delete one or more Cohesity users by SID. AD/IdP principal accounts are NOT deleted — only the Cohesity-side mapping.

name: list_roles
description: List Cohesity roles (both built-in and user-created). Each role bundles a set of privileges.

name: create_role
description: Create a custom Cohesity role with a specific set of privileges. Privilege names are the same strings shown in the cluster's Role configuration UI (e.g., PROTECTION_VIEW, PROTECTION_MODIFY, CLUSTER_VIEW).

name: update_role
description: Update a Cohesity role's privileges and/or description. The full privilege list must be supplied (this is a replace, not a merge).

name: delete_role
description: Delete a Cohesity role by name. Built-in roles cannot be deleted.

name: list_active_directories
description: List Active Directory domains joined to the Cohesity cluster

name: join_active_directory
description: Join the cluster to an Active Directory domain using admin credentials with rights to add a computer to the domain.

name: leave_active_directory
description: Remove an Active Directory from the Cohesity cluster

name: list_antivirus_groups
description: List antivirus service groups configured on the cluster. Each group bundles one or more ICAP-compliant antivirus servers.

name: create_antivirus_group
description: Create a new antivirus service group. Bundles one or more ICAP servers so the cluster can route on-access scans to them.

name: get_antivirus_group
description: Get a single antivirus service group by ID

name: update_antivirus_group
description: Update an antivirus service group's name, services, or state

name: delete_antivirus_group
description: Delete an antivirus service group

name: check_icap_connection
description: Probe an ICAP antivirus URI and return its current reachability status

name: list_infected_files
description: List files that an antivirus service has flagged as infected on cluster Views. Filter by view, time range, or quarantine state.

name: list_kms_configurations
description: List Key Management Systems (KMS) configured on the cluster. The InternalKms is always present; external types include AwsKms, KmipKms, IbmKms, and GcpKms.

name: add_aws_kms
description: Register an AWS KMS as an encryption key source. Used to encrypt storage domains or external targets.

name: add_kmip_kms
description: Register a KMIP-compliant KMS (e.g., Thales, Fortanix). Provide endpoint, port, CA cert, and client certs.

name: update_kms
description: Update an existing KMS configuration — assign additional storage domains or external targets, or rename it. Provider credentials cannot be changed via this method; delete and re-add to rotate credentials.

name: delete_kms
description: Delete a KMS configuration. The internal KMS (id 0) cannot be deleted.

name: get_external_target_encryption_key
description: Get the encryption key associated with an external target (e.g., AWS S3 bucket, Azure container). Used to verify which KMS is providing keys for archived data.

name: list_clone_tasks
description: List active and historical clone tasks (CloneVMs, CloneView, CloneAppView). Use filters to narrow by status or time range.

name: clone_view
description: Clone a file-services View into a new View. The clone is space-efficient (snapshot-backed). Useful for spinning up read/write test copies of production data.

name: delete_clone_task
description: Delete a restore clone task by ID. This tears down the clone and releases its space.
